message:
feat(pipeline): add multi-channel notification system

Implements notification support for pipeline workflow events with
three delivery channels: desktop, webhook, and agent mail.

Notification events:
- started:    Workflow execution begins
- completed:  Workflow finishes successfully
- failed:     Workflow terminates with error
- cancelled:  Workflow cancelled by user or signal
- step_error: Individual step failed (workflow may continue)

NotificationPayload includes:
- Event type and workflow name
- Run ID for correlation
- Session name (if applicable)
- Execution status (pending/running/completed/failed/cancelled)
- Duration, step counts (total/done/failed)
- Error details and failed step name on failure
- Timestamp for ordering

Delivery channels:

1. Desktop (ChannelDesktop):
   - macOS: Uses osascript to show native notification center alerts
   - Linux: Uses notify-send (libnotify) for desktop notifications
   - Includes workflow name in title, status details in body
   - Graceful no-op on missing tools

2. Webhook (ChannelWebhook):
   - POSTs JSON payload to configured URL
   - 10-second timeout with context cancellation
   - Returns error on non-2xx status or timeout
   - Suitable for Slack, Discord, PagerDuty integration

3. Agent Mail (ChannelMail):
   - Sends to configured agent recipient via agentmail.Client
   - Formats payload as structured markdown body
   - Enables agent-to-agent workflow event awareness
   - Requires valid project key and agent name

Usage:
  notifier := NewNotifier([]NotificationChannel{ChannelDesktop, ChannelWebhook})
  notifier.SetWebhook("https://hooks.slack.com/...")
  notifier.Notify(ctx, payload)

Test coverage:
- Payload serialization and field access
- Desktop command building per platform
- Webhook HTTP request formatting
- Mail message composition

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
